ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sea_level_reconstructions" (
  "reconstruction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sea level reconstruction */,
  "year_ad" INTEGER /* Year of the reconstruction (AD) */,
  "age_bp" INTEGER /* Age before present (BP) */,
  "median_elevation_m" REAL /* Median sea level elevation in meters */,
  "max_elevation_m" REAL /* Maximum sea level elevation in meters */,
  "min_elevation_m" REAL /* Minimum sea level elevation in meters */,
  "reconstruction_date" TEXT /* Date of the reconstruction */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or uncertainties associated with the data */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the sea level reconstruction data */,
  "uncertainty_range" REAL /* Uncertainty range associated with the median elevation */,
  "reconstruction_method" TEXT /* Method used for the sea level reconstruction */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version of the sea level reconstruction data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reconstruction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user (hashed for security) */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date and time of the user */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions of the user (e.g., read-only, read-write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "reconstruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sea level reconstruction being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the reconstruction */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the reconstruction was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the reconstruction */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent (browser, operating system, etc.) used to access the reconstruction */,
  "access_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_reconstruction_id FOREIGN KEY ("reconstruction_id") REFERENCES sea_level_reconstructions ("reconstruction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_validation" (
  "validation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data validation event */,
  "reconstruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sea level reconstruction being validated */,
  "validation_date" TEXT /* Date when the validation was performed */,
  "validation_result" TEXT /* Result of the validation (e.g., pass, fail) */,
  "validation_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the validation event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("validation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_validation_reconstruction_id FOREIGN KEY ("reconstruction_id") REFERENCES sea_level_reconstructions ("reconstruction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "version_history" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version of the sea level reconstruction data */,
  "reconstruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sea level reconstruction being versioned */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was created */,
  "version_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_version_history_reconstruction_id FOREIGN KEY ("reconstruction_id") REFERENCES sea_level_reconstructions ("reconstruction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
